answer:
In Iqbal's "Husn o Ishq," the speaker (personifying *ishq*, or Love) addresses *husn* (Beauty) directly, resolving their dynamic—often a site of torment, indifference, and paradox in Ghalib's ghazals—through mutual perfection and contentment.[^1] Where Ghalib's couplets (e.g., on indifferent Beauty inventing its own displays, or love trapped in endless longing and shame) perpetuate unresolved tension between the beloved's cruel allure and the lover's suffering,[^2] Iqbal declares harmony:

> From Your mere image, my astonishment is born;  
> Your Beauty is perfected, and my Love is perfect form.[^3]

> From Beauty, Love's own nature is spurred toward perfection;  
> From You, the saplings of my hopes find green direction.  
> My caravan has found contentment at its destination.[^4]

Beauty provides the catalyst ("lightning-flash") and object for Love's culmination, while Love perfects Beauty's form, ending in repose rather than strife.[^5] This echoes Iqbal's nearby poems on beauty's transience ("Haqeeqat-e-Husn") and splendor ("Jalwa-e-Husn"), but *Husn o Ishq* uniquely unites the pair in resolution.[^6][^7]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13 "Husn o Ishq" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/](#p-3bf497)).
[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 79 "Husn-e-beparwa kharidar-e-mata-e-jalwa hai" ([/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/79-husn-e-beparwa-kharidar-e-mata-e-jalwa-hai/translation/](#p-6fb822)); see also ch. 195 for love's consuming shame ([/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/195-waan-pahunch-kar-jo-ghash-aata-paye-ham-hai-hum-ko/translation/](#p-a97f3a)).
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13 ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/#p-0d606e).
[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13 ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/#p-7a49e3).
[^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 13 ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/13-husn-o-ishq/translation/#p-73eab8).
[^6]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 12 "Haqeeqat-e-Husn" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/12-haqeeqat-e-husn/translation/](#p-dfb149)).
[^7]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 15 "Jalwa-e-Husn" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/15-jalwa-e-husn/translation/](#p-d23c85)).
